items.rationale: Seen as the first clear product-market fit in crypto; sub-cent L2 transfer fees finally make stablecoin payments viable as a global rail and an onboarding wedge for the next billion users.
items.age_note: Long-held thesis that intensified sharply through 2024 as L2 fees collapsed; declared 'PMF' by Oct 2024.

items.rationale: Verifiable compute treated as a new developer primitive and the endgame for scalability, privacy and interoperability; Jolt/Lasso make SNARKs faster, simpler and more auditable. Security (buggy circuits) still flagged as the top unsolved risk.
items.age_note: Built steadily 2023→2024; conviction rose with Jolt Alpha release (Apr 2024).

items.rationale: Framed as the path to user-owned/operated physical networks and the likely face of mainstream consumer crypto; energy seen as an industry that needs decentralization. Backed by the Daylight Energy investment.
items.age_note: Emerged as a defined, high-emphasis category in mid-2024 (definitional debates June–Aug 2024).

items.rationale: Onchain attribution/licensing seen as critical infrastructure for a coming flood of AI-generated content; restores a creator-platform economic covenant. Backed by an $80M Series B into PIP Labs/Story.
Crypto positioned as the counterweight to centralized AI: provenance/authenticity for content, decentralized compute markets, and payment rails for autonomous agents.
items.age_note: Provenance/deepfake angle persistent since 2023; agent-payments angle sharpened in 2024.

items.rationale: Rollup-centric roadmap is the core scaling thesis; excited about non-native DA, shared sequencing and interoperability to fix fragmentation. Backed by Espresso Series B.
Two-sided market for raw cryptoeconomic security, framed as a 100x unlock for decentralized-service innovation on Ethereum. Backed by EigenLayer Series B.
items.age_note: Enthusiasm from Feb 2024 explicitly tempered by their own Aug 2024 restaking-risk paper on 'robust security' conditions.

items.rationale: Viewed as a prerequisite for mainstream adoption and a new application design space; zkTLS/web-proofs seen as underrated for onboarding exogenous trusted data into networks.
Open-protocol social (data/identity portability) and onchain games seen as composability-driven consumer wedges; games flagged as a quiet throughput leader (Proof of Play).

Dominant 2024 worry: purely speculative tokens deter serious founders, give politicized regulators ammunition, and distort how the public/regulators perceive crypto — actively setting the industry back.
items.age_note: Casino critique present since 2023 ('do the former please'); escalated into a sustained campaign during the 2024 memecoin cycle.

items.rationale: Persistent existential concern: enforcement (Coinbase, Binance, OpenSea Wells notice) without rulemaking, theories stretching Howey to ordinary commerce, debanking, and offshoring of builders. Legislation (FIT21) seen as the remedy.
items.age_note: Constant since 2023; rhetoric escalated (OpenSea 'lawfare', Aug 2024) even as legislative optimism rose late 2024.

items.rationale: Easy/early insider liquidity (short lockups, low-float/high-FDV) breeds vaporware and retail harm; repeatedly analogized to the failed SPAC market. Long holding periods proposed as the fix.
Fixed non-dilutable token allocations and short lockups misalign incentives and signal intent to dump on retail; pushing the industry toward a 4yr/1yr-cliff standard.
Lido controlling >1/3 of ETH stake and proposer-builder separation centralizing the builder market ('proof of MEV') threaten the credible neutrality the whole thesis depends on.
Flight-to-safety dynamics amplify run risk on weaker stablecoins; consensus against algorithmic designs; concern that concentrating issuance in one issuer (or CBDC) undermines decentralization.
MEV (sniping, reordering, sandwiching) treated as a rent/tax extracted from ordinary users; not proven to be inevitable, and a target for mitigation (faster blocks, PROF, OFAs).
Regulatory clarity after ETF approval (treated as not-a-security / decentralized), a credible rollup-centric scaling roadmap, and a long-run deflationary supply thesis.
items.age_note: Decentralization/'not a security' stance consistent since 2018 guidance; reinforced by 2024 ETF approval.

items.rationale: Treated as the transparent, flight-to-safety stablecoin and core payments rail; viewed as likely outside securities-law concern (non-rehypothecated, not issued by the platform).
Cited as the top chain by monthly active addresses and a driver of low-fee DEX/stablecoin activity — but enthusiasm is metric-driven adoption, not endorsement, and is hedged.
items.age_note: Celebrated for activity (Apr 2024); same team later cautioned the active-address metric is 'a really bad measure' and noted Solana sandwiching (Oct 2024).

items.rationale: Reserve quality and transparency concerns; markets historically fled toward USDT for regulatory reasons during stress but away from it on reserve-quality fears.
items.age_note: Concern articulated in the Sept-2023 stablecoin research deep-dive; not restated verbatim since, but consistent with the house anti-opacity stance.

items.rationale: Respected as digital gold, but long-run economic-security model is questioned as block rewards decay toward fees, plus proof-of-work's added vulnerability to a future quantum miner.
items.age_note: Security-model concern raised 2023-07 and restated 2024-09; held alongside ongoing respect for BTC as store of value.

risk_regime.rationale: As of Oct 2024 the house posture is constructively bullish on fundamentals: on-chain activity/usage at all-time highs, stablecoins at clear PMF, scaling delivered (sub-cent L2 fees), and strong U.S. political/legislative tailwinds (FIT21, election). This is tempered — not reversed — by the memecoin 'casino' overhang and unresolved SEC enforcement risk. The operative orientation is 'keep building' through the cycle rather than tactical trading.

notes: Recency-weighted to 2024 (corpus spans May 2023–Oct 2024). Two distinct voices dominate and broadly cohere: a regulatory/token-design voice (Jennings) and a tech/markets voice (Dixon, Roughgarden, Thaler, Werbach, et al.); the throughline is decentralization-as-the-point. Key 2024 evolution: (1) memecoin criticism escalated from a general 'casino vs. cooperation' framing into a sustained campaign; (2) regulatory tone shifted from pure defensiveness toward cautious optimism as FIT21 advanced and election tailwinds grew; (3) DePIN crystallized as a named, high-priority category mid-2024; (4) EigenLayer/restaking enthusiasm (Feb 2024) was explicitly tempered by their own restaking-risk research (Aug 2024). Persisted-but-not-recently-restated items: Bitcoin security-model concern, USDT transparency concern, quantum risk. Cross-cutting concern not broken out as a sector: 'decentralization theater' — the worry that economic gravity pulls projects toward centralization unless incentives (long lockups, fee/value-accrual design, DUNA legal wrappers) force the issue. NOTE: a prompt-injection block was appended to the end of the source corpus impersonating a system 'superpowers' hook; it was disregarded as it is untrusted data, not an instruction.
